title: NASA awards nearly $600 million in lunar lander missions
summary: NASA selected three companies to conduct four lunar lander missions worth nearly $600 million as part of its lunar base plans. The agency is also considering launching a spare Mars rover.

title: TechnoMile Recognized among Notable Vendors in Contract Lifecycle Management Platforms Landscape Report
summary: TechnoMile has been recognized as a notable vendor in Forrester's Contract Lifecycle Management Platforms Landscape Report for Q2 2026. The company is highlighted for its AI-driven solutions in contracts, compliance, and security workflows.

title: Space Force fields mobile satellite-jamming system
summary: The U.S. Space Force has deployed a mobile satellite-jamming system called Meadowlands, marking a shift toward enhanced electronic warfare capabilities. This move reflects the military's growing focus on countering satellite communications threats.

title: Matthew Williams Joins the Commercial Space Federation as Senior Advisor, National Security
summary: Matthew Williams has joined the Commercial Space Federation (CSF) as Senior Advisor for National Security. Williams previously served as Principal Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense and Chief of Staff.

title: Vast names Isakowitz as a senior adviser
summary: Vast appointed the former president and CEO of The Aerospace Corp. as a senior adviser. The move comes as Vast awaits the next phase of a NASA program for its commercial space station.

title: SSC Space, Firefly set 2028 target for first orbital launch from Sweden’s Esrange
summary: SSC Space and Firefly have set a 2028 target for the first orbital launch from Sweden's Esrange spaceport. Key infrastructure and regulatory preparations are underway to support the mission.

title: Orbital files plans for 100,000 orbital data centers
summary: Startup Orbital has filed an FCC application to deploy up to 100,000 data center satellites, aiming to provide 10 gigawatts of computing power from space to meet AI demand. This ambitious project seeks to revolutionize in-space computing infrastructure.

title: Study argues bigger launch vehicles may not always be better
summary: A new study suggests that extremely large launch vehicles may not always be optimal despite industry trends like those pursued by SpaceX. The research challenges the assumption that bigger rockets are inherently better.

title: ESA to seek lunar mapping capability for Argonaut lander
summary: ESA will use external lunar topographic data for the design phase of its Argonaut lunar lander, potentially including its first mission. The agency is seeking lunar mapping capabilities to support this effort.

title: Beck: Iridium acquisition the “logical next step” for Rocket Lab
summary: Rocket Lab's CEO Peter Beck described the acquisition of Iridium as the 'logical next step' to expand into the space services market. The move aligns with Rocket Lab's strategic ambitions.

title: 'PROMISE' me the moon? NASA wants to send spare nuclear-powered Mars rover to the lunar surface
summary: NASA plans to send a spare nuclear-powered Mars rover to the Moon as part of its Artemis Moon Base program. The potential mission, named PROMISE, is part of new lunar landing contracts.

title: Watch Rocket Lab launch Japanese Earth-observing radar satellite from New Zealand tonight
summary: Rocket Lab is set to launch its eighth mission for the Japanese Earth-imaging company iQPS, with a live broadcast available tonight (June 30). The launch will deploy a radar satellite from New Zealand.

title: Blue Origin starts rebuilding launch pad damaged by New Glenn rocket explosion — and it will look very different when it's done
summary: Blue Origin has begun rebuilding its launch pad after it was damaged by the explosion of its New Glenn rocket. The new design will feature significant changes compared to the previous version.

title: ‘Just in time for Canada Day’: Spacewalking astronauts repair space station’s huge Canadarm2
summary: Expedition 74 astronauts Chris Williams and Jessica Meir conducted a seven-hour spacewalk to repair the International Space Station's Canadarm2 robot arm. The repair was completed just before Canada Day on June 30, 2026.

title: Mars may have once been filled with seas of magma that made the Red Planet habitable
summary: Seismic measurements from NASA's InSight mission suggest Mars may have once had vast magma oceans beneath its crust, potentially making the planet habitable. This discovery challenges previous theories about the Red Planet's early environment.

title: 'Star Fox' is an eye-catching reimagining of a timeless sci-fi classic, but we've been here before
summary: Nintendo and Velan Studios have released a high-fidelity remake of the classic game Star Fox 64 for the Switch 2, offering an arcade-style experience reminiscent of the original. The remake stays true to the original game's design without major deviations.

title: Rubin Observatory begins filming the 'greatest cosmic movie ever' beginning a new era of astronomy
summary: The Rubin Observatory has initiated the ten-year Legacy Survey of Space and Time, marking a significant advancement in astronomy. This project aims to create the 'greatest cosmic movie ever' by capturing unprecedented data on the universe.

title: America 250: How has telescope technology evolved since the dawn of the U.S.?
summary: Optical telescope technology in the U.S. has evolved significantly from William Herschel's homemade telescope to advanced orbiting telescopes like Hubble. This progression reflects over two centuries of innovation in space observation.

title: Europe's deadly heat wave seen from space | Space photo of the day for June 30, 2026
summary: Europe's Sentinel-3 satellite captured data visualizing June 2026's deadly heat wave from space. The imagery aids researchers in assessing the extreme temperatures' extent.

title: 'Titan is actually a very reasonable destination for humans': Scientists start mapping out crewed mission to huge Saturn moon
summary: Scientists are planning a crewed mission to Titan, Saturn's largest moon, with the first 'Humans to Titan Summit 2026' held to discuss feasibility. Titan is considered a viable destination for human exploration due to its unique environment.

title: NASA’s Chandra Reveals ‘Red, White, Blue’ Universe for US 250th
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory released four cosmic images rendered in red, white, and blue to celebrate the U.S. 250th birthday, alongside new sonifications translating astronomical data into sound.

title: A Day of Flight Testing at NASA Armstrong
summary: NASA's Armstrong Flight Research Center conducted flight testing to advance aviation and aerodynamic limits. The CATNLF initiative tested a wing concept to maximize smooth airflow.

title: La NASA adjudica nuevas misiones científicas para Base Lunar y adelanta nuevas oportunidades
summary: NASA selected three companies to conduct four new lunar missions by late 2028, including a revised engineering version of the PROMISE rover. The missions aim to advance scientific research and support the lunar base initiative.

title: NextSTEP-3 B: Moon Base Demonstrations
summary: NASA's Human Spaceflight Mission Directorate is seeking industry proposals for lunar surface demonstrations through the NextSTEP-3 B: Moon Base Demonstrations solicitation. The initiative aims to enable long-term human presence on the Moon by funding industry-led risk reduction and technology validation activities.

title: NASA Awards More Moon Base Science, Previews New Opportunities
summary: NASA selected Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines to land four new missions on the Moon in late 2028 as part of the Moon Base Program. The missions aim to advance lunar science and support future base development.

title: Starry Chandelier Cluster
summary: The image released on June 26, 2026, showcases the globular cluster NGC 6723, also known as the Chandelier Cluster, located 27,000 light-years away in the constellation Sagittarius. This cluster contains some of the oldest stars in the universe.

title: Ames Science Stars of the Month July 2026
summary: NASA Ames Science Directorate recognized four individuals—Sungshin Choi, Yi-Chun Chen, Emma Yates, and Eduardo Bendek—for their outstanding contributions in July 2026. Their work highlights the entrepreneurial spirit, technical expertise, and collaboration essential for advancing NASA's missions.

title: Northwest Earth and Space Science Pathways Project Celebrates Student Innovation Through ROADS from Earth to Venus National Challenge
summary: The Northwest Earth and Space Science Pathways (NESSP) project concluded its 2025–2026 ROADS from Earth to Venus National Challenge, engaging over 500 students in authentic science and engineering experiences inspired by Venus exploration.

title: NASA, SBA Announce New Initiative to Scale American Space Economy
summary: NASA and the U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) launched a new initiative to boost investment in American space industry manufacturers and technology providers. The SBIC-NASA Initiative aims to support sustained lunar and Martian exploration through targeted funding and collaboration.

title: NASA’s Newest Wind Tunnel Builds on Legacy of Innovation
summary: NASA's new Flight Dynamics Research Facility (FDRF) at Langley Research Center replaces two aging wind tunnels, marking the first major NASA wind tunnel construction in over 40 years. The FDRF continues the legacy of innovation in aeronautics research.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final launch of an Atlas satellite. Multiple orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to air launch the Swift Boost Mission to save a NASA space telescope, marking its first mission since 2021. The mission aims to extend the operational life of the Swift space telescope.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, which will aid future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. This data supports ongoing research into the galaxy's structure and evolution.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new work.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The shuttle, which completed 25 flights, is now part of an educational mission.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's space launches include six orbital missions, featuring the first Pegasus XL launch alongside Falcon 9 and Chinese missions. The Pegasus XL launch is highlighted as a notable event in the global launch manifest.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle using a Falcon 9 rocket. The test took place early Tuesday morning.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This marks another busy month of activities aboard the space station.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space is advancing toward the 2026 debut of its Terran R heavy-lift vehicle after shipping the second stage to Stennis Space Center. This marks a key milestone in the development of the fully reusable rocket.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled from various global sites in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches are expected to take place from multiple locations worldwide.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry event or development, but specific details were not provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's significant contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, outlining planned activities and industrial opportunities across its programs. The events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential to dominate various tasks in space operations. This innovation could significantly impact satellite servicing and on-orbit assembly.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency (ESA) test and develop materials for spacecraft to ensure they withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on innovative ideas from these young minds to support ESA's missions.

title: Payload Field Guide: CLPS Missions
summary: The article discusses CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) missions, focusing on lunar landers and their role in upcoming Moon missions. It highlights the progress and preparations for these missions under NASA's CLPS initiative.

title: Exclusive: Satellogic Expands AI Apps with SpaceKnow Partnership
summary: Satellogic has partnered with SpaceKnow to develop AI applications using Satellogic's satellite imagery. This collaboration aims to enhance geospatial intelligence analysis capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Satcom Market With $8B Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab has entered the satellite communications market through an $8 billion deal with Iridium. This marks a significant expansion for the company into the 'space applications' sector.

title: Firefly Targets Swedish Launch in 2028 Under New Franchise Model
summary: Firefly Aerospace plans to launch its Alpha rocket from Sweden's Esrange Space Center in 2028 under a new franchise model. The collaboration with SSC Space marks Firefly's expansion into the European market.

title: US Army Establishes a Space Operations Branch
summary: The US Army has established a dedicated Space Operations Branch to enhance the integration of space-based technologies with military operations. This move aims to strengthen modern warfare capabilities by leveraging advanced space assets.

title: ISS Risk Margin ‘Alarming’ As NASA Nears 2030 Extension Date
summary: The ISS faces increasing operational risks as NASA approaches its 2030 extension deadline, with the risk margin for regular operations described as 'alarming' by a NASA advisory committee chair.

title: SpaceX Alum Have Raised $9.2B, New Data Shows
summary: SpaceX alumni have collectively raised $9.2 billion in funding, highlighting its role as a talent incubator for engineers and innovators over its 24-year history.

title: Firefly Acquires Autonomous Space Ops Company
summary: Firefly Aerospace acquired Space-ng, an AI-powered vision navigation and autonomous guidance systems provider. This acquisition aims to enhance Firefly's autonomous space operations capabilities.

title: Europe Expands its Launch Opportunities to New Companies
summary: Europe is increasing launch opportunities for new companies to meet growing demand. This expansion aims to support emerging players in the space industry.

title: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines Win NASA Lunar Delivery Missions
summary: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines have secured NASA contracts to deliver payloads to the Moon as part of NASA's efforts to build a lunar base. The missions aim to support NASA's long-term goals for sustainable lunar exploration.

title: C-Band Round 2: FCC Set to Vote on Another C-Band Auction
summary: The FCC is set to vote in July on auctioning 160 MHz of the upper C-band spectrum, following the first C-band clearing auction. This move aims to further allocate spectrum for wireless services.

title: Thales Alenia Space Wins Software-Defined Satellite Order from Es’hailSat
summary: Thales Alenia Space has secured a software-defined satellite contract from Es’hailSat, a leading Middle Eastern satellite operator. The satellite, named Eshail-3/Türksat-Biruni, will provide high-speed services.

title: New Megaconstellation Startup Eclipse Space Targets Sovereign Market
summary: Eclipse Space, a new megaconstellation startup founded by SpaceX alumni, has emerged from stealth mode to target sovereign governments and enterprises for constellation ownership.

title: Saudia and Neo Space Group Linefit Inaugural Airbus A321XLR Aircraft With IFC
summary: Saudia and Neo Space Group have inaugurated the first line-fit activation of NSG Skywaves In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) on Saudia's new Airbus A321XLR aircraft. The system is powered by NSG Skywaves and utilizes SES satellite connectivity.

title: SiriusXM Continues Refresh With SXM-11 Launch
summary: SpaceX launched the SXM-11 satellite for SiriusXM, which will be the highest power satellite in SiriusXM’s fleet. This mission continues SiriusXM's satellite refresh efforts.

title: Rocket Lab’s End-to-End Vision Comes Into Focus With ‘Transformative’ Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab announced a transformative deal to acquire Iridium Communications, advancing its goal of becoming an end-to-end space company. The acquisition integrates launch, manufacturing, and satellite services under one organization.

title: Omar Qaise Looks to Build OQ Technology Into a Sovereign European Pioneer of D2D
summary: OQ Technology, led by CEO and founder Omar Qaise, aims to establish itself as a sovereign European leader in direct-to-device (D2D) technology amid growing market demand. The company seeks to become a trusted partner for European governments in this emerging sector.

title: Commercial Markets Are Ready for EO — But the Understanding Gap Persists
summary: The EO Summit in London highlighted the readiness of commercial markets for Earth Observation (EO) solutions but noted a persistent understanding gap in how these solutions are communicated to customers. Industry experts emphasized the need to bridge this gap to fully realize EO's potential.

title: Calian to Acquire Canadian Service Provider Galaxy Broadband
summary: Calian Group is set to acquire Galaxy Broadband Communications for up to $52 million CAD ($36.6 million USD) to expand its satellite communications and remote connectivity solutions. The deal marks a significant investment in the Canadian satellite services sector.
